Electronic FOIA Amendments get mixed review

Bob Anderson Freedom of Information Center November 1996
TechnologyInvestigative MethodsData & Databases

The Electronic Freedom of Information Act Amendments of 1996 integrate the FOIA into the digital age. Signed into law by President Clinton on October 2, the amendments ensure that electronic records a...

The Electronic Freedom of Information Act Amendments of 1996 integrate the FOIA into the digital age. Signed into law by President Clinton on October 2, the amendments ensure that electronic records are accessible to the public. The law provides for public access to electronic formats such as computer tapes and CDs, allows requesters to get information in any specified format, mandates online availability of specific records by agencies, and changes the response time for FOIA requests to 20 days.

While some criticize the amendments' limited provisions, experts like Paul McMasters see potential benefits for journalists, especially in accessing databases.
